definition.name: self_adjoint_part
definition.definition: For a *-algebra M (or a von Neumann algebra, C*-algebra, etc.), the self-adjoint part M^{sa} = {T ∈ M : T = T*} is the real subspace of self-adjoint (Hermitian) elements. It is a real vector space (not a complex subspace, since iT is not self-adjoint when T is). Every element a ∈ M decomposes uniquely as a = Re(a) + i·Im(a) with Re(a) = (a+a*)/2, Im(a) = (a-a*)/(2i) both in M^{sa}, so dim_R(M^{sa}) = dim_C(M) when M is finite-dimensional. For example: dim_R(M_n(C)^{sa}) = n² (the space of n×n Hermitian matrices). For a commutant decomposition π_φ(A)' ≅ ⊕_{j=1}^k M_{n_j}(C), we have dim_R(π_φ(A)'^{sa}) = Σ_{j=1}^k n_j².
